channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Next door (28) have broken the pavement it’s very dangerous, 14th April
item.description:
Our neighbour is having work on their house and the grabber truck has ruined the pavement made it sink in and very dangerous! My elderly mother fell on it and our neighbour up the road who has dementia fell too it’s awful! Please fix but charge the business truck for this please
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 38, Pickhurst Park, Hayes, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Re: Tree Damage – Formal Escalation Notice, 14th April
item.description:
Dear Sir/Madam, Thank you for your response dated 14.4.26 at 15:12 PM. However, I must strongly challenge the conclusion that this issue does not meet the threshold for action. This is not a minor tree-related issue such as leaf fall or general maintenance. As clearly shown in the photographs provided, large trees/branches have collapsed and caused significant structural damage to my fence and property. This situation presents: - A serious safety hazard due to unstable and fallen trees - An ongoing risk of further damage or injury - Actual property damage, not a potential or minor inconvenience Given the severity of the situation, I formally request: 1. An urgent re-inspection of the site 2. Written confirmation of ownership and responsibility for the trees 3. Immediate action to remove hazardous trees and debris 4. Information on how to proceed with a claim for the damage caused If this matter continues to be dismissed, I will be escalating this as a formal complaint and referring the matter to the Local Government and Social Care Ombudsman and local MP for independent review, on the basis that the response provided does not reflect the seriousness of the damage or associated risk. I would appreciate a prompt response as this could have literally fallen on someone which could cause life threatening injuries and then what? id have to be a claim against the council to be heard and rightfully compensated which i will absolutely do as it is within my rights as under common law negligence, the council has a duty to maintain trees under its control and prevent foreseeable damage to neighbouring property. As well as under the Occupiers’ Liability (duty over land) which states : Whoever controls the land (in this case this is the council) must ensure it’s reasonably safe. That includes trees and structures on the land And as the occupier of the land, the council has a responsibility to ensure it is reasonably safe and does not pose a hazard to neighbouring property ( which is MY property). The continued presence of unstable trees causing damage constitutes a private nuisance affecting my property. Also the Highways Act 1980 applies to this situation as this is near a road . If those trees are by a road the Highways Act 1980 can apply and then the council must ensure roads are safe and that includes removing dangers like unsafe trees so under the Highways Act 1980, the council has a responsibility to address hazards arising from roadside trees. I also have no issues making a claim against the counsel through National Highways.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Ray Lamb Way, Slade Green, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Faulty playground equipment, 14th April
item.description:
There is a circuit of mushrooms right next to the cafe and the top of one is completely loose. My daughter was jumping on them on Sunday the 12th April, and fell over when she got to the loose one, fracturing her elbow. We went back today, after the hospital appointment, to talk to someone in the cafe and check if the equipment was still loose and they told us that we had to report the issue here. The top of the mushroom is still loose and can come off, as my daughter shows in the picture.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Stone Park Avenue, Eden Park, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Glass in the road by the curb, 14th April
item.description:
Glass in road by curb next to florence Road street sign. Looks like a smashed bottle.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Florence Road, Elmers End, Greater London

A lot of litter on this side of the road, mostly on the pavement but some also in the road Thank you

item.title: Lots of rubbish in the alley way, 14th April
item.description:
This has been like this for months. People just leave their rubbish and old house belongings and dump it all. It's dangerous as children live in this area. School children walk through the alley and go into the car park as a short cut.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Gloucester Parade, Blackfen, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Abandoned car, 14th April
item.description:
Car has been in disabled spot for several months. Tyres are flat and has not been attended to in months
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Homerton Road, London

item.description:
Someone dumped a cabinet next to the bins. Can this be collected please

item.title: Rubbish, 14th April
item.description:
Rubbish is still at the back of the planter boxes. Please can it be taken away & the area be cleaned & kept with no rubbish let to build up.

item.title: Graffiti on wall in Cherry Garden park, 14th April
item.description:
There is new graffiti on wall in Cherry Garden park, two lots of graffiti have appeared over the weekend. Please clean it off before move happens - thanks

item.title: Fly tipping and abandoned bikes, 14th April
item.description:
Someone has left several bags of rubbish, including what appears to be small appliances near the junction box. This has been there for approximately a week. There are also two broken bikes slightly further down the street which have appeared over the last few days.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Howarth Road, Abbey Wood

item.title: Clearance and replanting, 14th April
item.description:
Could this area be reviewed for clearance and replanting, as it currently appears overgrown and untidy, to improve its appearance?

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Kirkland Walk, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Car has been dumped here for over a year, 14th April
item.description:
Many residents are getting parking tickets because they are unable to use the parking spaces due to dumped cars which have broken windows and never been moved.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Barnfield Gardens, Plumstead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Footway obstruction caused by construction signage, 14th April
item.description:
The attached photograph shows two highway safety concerns on the Lubbock Road footway associated with the AVF Beechbrook House construction site. 1. A temporary construction sign has been placed on the only usable pedestrian footway, narrowing the available walking route. 2. Another temporary sign has been left leaning against private property at the edge of the opposite footway. The legs are not extended, increasing the risk of the sign falling into the pedestrian route. These items obstruct and compromise pedestrian safety, especially for pushchair and wheelchair users who require the full clear width of the pavement. Please investigate this as a highway safety issue, confirm what action will be taken to remove the obstruction and ensure temporary signage is positioned and secured safely, and remind AVF of their obligations in relation to highway safety. Photograph attached - captured 14 April 2026 @ 19:28

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Lubbock Road, Elmstead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 14 or more vehicles all parked fully on the pavements at alltimes of th day and night making pedestrians walk in the road. They even put their bins out when they leave to reserve their ''' spaces '., 14th April

Residents all insist on parking all on the pavements

item.title: Hire bikes blocking pavement, 14th April
item.description:
Despite there being a designated hire bike parking bay on the road and within 20m this hire company have no regard to stop customers abandoning their bikes in an area which is constantly navigated by blind and partially sighted patients visiting Moorfields Eye Hospital.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 11-43, Bath Street, Finsbury, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Newbury road street lights too dim, 14th April
item.description:
The street lights at Newbury Road are too dim and we can barely see the sidewalks / pavement because it’s too dark. I live at 25 Newbury road and I did install a sensor light in front of my house as it was too dark before and I felt unsafe.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Newbury Road, Shortlands, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
